rationale: Fresh US-Iran military escalation (strikes confirmed as of July 10, 2026) is driving a live risk-off bid for USD and suppressing AUD via safe-haven flows and oil-price headwinds on Australia's terms of trade; AUD is already approaching 3-month lows near 0.694 and sits below its 50d SMA, suggesting near-term momentum is down. RBA's easing cycle and a Fed constrained by higher oil-driven CPI reinforce the rate-differential drag on AUD. The main reversal risk is rapid Iran de-escalation or a China stimulus surprise.
key_drivers: US-Iran military escalation → USD safe-haven bid
Oil price spike → AUD terms-of-trade headwind
RBA easing cycle compressing AUD-USD rate differential
Fed cut repricing delayed by oil/inflation risk
AUD below 50d SMA, near 3-month lows — bearish momentum
China demand uncertainty in risk-off environment
